name: Cafe Au Lait Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon instant espresso coffee powder or regular coffee crystals
recipeInstructions:
1. Crust: In a mixing bowl, combine the crushed chocolate wafers and melted margarine or butter. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom and up the sides of an ungreased 8-inch springform pan. Place the pan on a baking sheet. Chill until ready.
2. In a small saucepan, combine the chopped semisweet chocolate, water, and instant espresso (or regular coffee crystals). Cook over low heat, stirring occasionally until the chocolate melts. Remove from heat and stir in the coffee liqueur or water. Allow to cool.
3. In a large mixer bowl, beat the cream cheese, sugar, all-purpose flour, and vanilla with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth. Add the eggs one at a time, beating on low speed just until incorporated. Do not overbeat.
4. Reserve 2 cups of the cream cheese mixture. Cover and chill.
5. Stir the cooled chocolate-coffee mixture into the remaining cream cheese mixture, stirring just until combined. Pour the chocolate mixture into the crust.
6. Bake in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 30 minutes or until the sides are set (the center will remain soft). Remove the reserved mixture from the refrigerator and gently pour it over the inside edge of the chocolate mixture (where the chocolate mixture is set). Spread evenly over the entire surface.
7. Continue baking the cheesecake for 20-25 more minutes or until the center appears nearly set when gently shaken. Allow to cool for 10 minutes on a wire rack.
8. Loosen the sides of the cheesecake from the pan and allow to cool for another 30 minutes. Remove the sides of the pan and let the cheesecake cool completely.
9. Chill the cheesecake for 4-24 hours before serving. Store any remaining cheesecake, covered in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
10. To Freeze: Place the cooled cheesecake, uncovered, in the freezer for about 1 hour until firm. Remove the bottom pan and transfer the cheesecake to a large freezer bag or container. Seal, label, and freeze up to 3 months. To serve, remove the cheesecake from the freezer and loosely cover it. Thaw for 24 hours in the refrigerator.
